site.name: LIREI
site.fullname: Laboratory of Innovation and Research in Intelligent Energy
site.description: LIREI is a multidisciplinary team of the Hydrogen Research Institute (UQTR) developing solutions for intelligent energy management.

footer.affiliation: Member of the Hydrogen Research Institute (IRH)
footer.address: 3351 boul. des Forges, C.P. 500, Trois-Rivières (Québec) G9A 5H7, Canada

home.intro: LIREI is a multidisciplinary team of researchers, members of the Hydrogen Research Institute (IRH) at the Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières. Since 1999, we have been working to optimize energy use in buildings and vehicles, with a particular interest in hydrogen and renewable energy.

lab.title: The laboratory
lab.lead: A centre of innovation and exploration at the heart of the Hydrogen Research Institute, where a multidisciplinary team designs tomorrow's intelligent energy solutions.
lab.intro1: LIREI brings together professors, research professionals and graduate students around a shared mission: proposing solutions for intelligent energy management, from the power grid to buildings and vehicles. Our work combines optimization, machine learning, modeling and experimentation on real-world data.
lab.history1: The Hydrogen Research Institute (IRH) at UQTR was publicly inaugurated in 1996. Three years later, in 1999, LIREI was created as a multidisciplinary team within the Institute, with the mission of proposing solutions to manage energy intelligently.
lab.history2: Today, the laboratory pursues its activities in Trois-Rivières and benefits from the new research facilities of UQTR's Pellerin-Marmen building.
lab.programsLead: Our activities are organized around three major programs that structure the laboratory's doctoral projects and collaborations.
lab.infraLead: LIREI provides its members with state-of-the-art facilities and unique methodological know-how.

residential.title: Residential test benches
residential.desc: Instrumentation of buildings and loads (heating, water heaters, thermal storage) to validate management strategies under real cold-climate conditions.

simulation.title: Simulation platforms
simulation.desc: Co-simulation of distribution and communication networks (OpenDSS, multi-agent systems) to test transactive mechanisms at scale.

data.title: Data and learning
data.desc: Real-world consumption, charging and occupancy datasets, and machine-learning tools for energy modeling and forecasting.

optimization.title: Optimization and computing
optimization.desc: Convex and stochastic optimization methods and distributed computing for the sizing and optimal operation of energy resources.

lab.env1: The laboratory offers an inclusive and innovative environment that fosters interdisciplinary collaboration, mentorship and scientific dissemination. Graduate students benefit from supervision conducive to professional development and publication.
lab.env2: LIREI's work attracts strong industrial interest in Quebec: our network of partners is the main source of project funding.
lab.ctaText: LIREI is always looking for motivated graduate students interested in intelligent energy management.

research.title: Research
research.lead: LIREI structures its work around three major research programs, expressed through doctoral projects and collaborations, and spanning a broad range of areas of expertise.
research.axesLead: Our programs build on a foundation of cross-cutting technical expertise.

smartgrids.title: Smart grids and simulation platforms
smartgrids.desc: Design and simulation of smart power grids that use computing to better manage consumption and energy exchanges between suppliers and consumers. Two-level coordination and control of distributed systems.

residential.title: Residential energy management
residential.desc: Energy management systems for residential buildings: demand response, thermal storage, consumption monitoring and occupant comfort.
residential.tags: demand response, thermal storage, energy efficiency

ml.title: Machine learning and consumption monitoring
ml.desc: Application of machine learning to non-intrusive load monitoring, occupancy detection and stochastic modeling of electrical loads.
ml.tags: machine learning, occupancy detection, stochastic modeling

hydrogen.title: Hydrogen and fuel cells
hydrogen.desc: Integration of hydrogen and fuel cells into energy systems, in synergy with renewable energy and the work of the Hydrogen Research Institute.

flexibility.title: Flexibility markets and transactive energy
flexibility.desc: Transactive energy mechanisms and local flexibility markets: convex optimization, economic analysis, equity and multi-agent coordination of distributed energy resources.
flexibility.tags: transactive energy, convex optimization, flexibility markets, multi-agent

ev.title: Electric vehicles and smart mobility
ev.desc: Optimization of energy use in electric and hydrogen vehicles, and integration of vehicles into the grid as flexibility providers.
ev.tags: electric vehicles, smart charging, sustainable mobility

team.title: The team
team.lead: A multidisciplinary team of professors, research professionals and graduate students.

pubs.title: Publications
pubs.lead: Recent scientific contributions by LIREI members.
pubs.noResults: No publications match the selected filters.

news.title: News
news.lead: The latest news from the laboratory, the Hydrogen Research Institute and our partners.

projects.title: Projects
projects.lead: Overview of the research projects carried out at the laboratory with our partners.
projects.programsLead: Our work is organized around three major research programs. Each program brings together doctoral projects and collaborations that explore its different facets.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: For any question about our research activities or to reach the team.
contact.partnersDesc: LIREI's work is made possible through the collaboration of institutional and industrial partners.
contact.recruitText: LIREI is always looking for motivated graduate students (master's, PhD, postdoc) interested in intelligent energy management. Write to us to discuss research opportunities.
